page: Microsoft Azure D48as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv4 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. The configuration features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core, supported by 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 393 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not bundle any dedicated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and computational capability. The server achieves a strong rating in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance (64921.67) and single-core performance (2707.29). PassMark testing places its memory score in the top tier (3094.05), while its CPU score (52071.3) and encryption test (51638.79) rank in the top 25%. Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 is also strong at 218.63 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth benchmarks for both small and large blocks fall into the average tier, and large-model LLM text generation (70B model) is weak at 2.78 tokens/sec, highlighting a performance tradeoff for massive parameter models without GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48as_v4 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads. The dedicated AMD EPYC processor and high core count provide solid value for multi-threaded applications without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. This hardware profile is suited for multi-threaded data processing, image processing, software compilation, and general-purpose web serving or database operations.
description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv4 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It features 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores, 192.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core), and 393 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance delivers strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core score of 64921.67 and a top-tier PassMark memory score. While memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average range, the server excels in multi-threaded decompression and image processing. Lacking a GPU, it exhibits weak performance on large-scale LLM text generation. This server is suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads requiring a balanced CPU-to-memory ratio.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor, delivering strong multi-core and top-tier memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, offering strong multi-core performance.
tagline: A balanced 48-vCPU virtual machine with dedicated AMD EPYC processing and 192 GB RAM for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
393 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-core CPU and top-tier PassMark memory benchmark performance
Suited for multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and web serving
